summary: A reference-architecture + seed matrix for building an exhaustive, deduplicated catalogue of counterintelligence (CI) red-cell methods.
final_use_case: It turns analytic methods into reusable instruments with inputs, steps, failure modes, interpretation rules, and reviewer actions.

instruments.instrument_name: CI red-cell methods reference architecture (post-integrity-correction rebuild) Room Task Model

instruments.instrument_name: CI red-cell methods reference architecture (post-integrity-correction rebuild) Data Schema And Persistence Plan
instruments.input: entities, records, IDs, tables, JSON structures, indexes, and state transitions
instruments.operation: Intelligence tradecraft, counterintelligence, and analytic method. Use source library intelligence titles as method authority and intelligence-domain stressors. instruments.instrument_name: CI red-cell methods reference architecture (post-integrity-correction rebuild) Integration Path
instruments.input: frontend, backend, storage, retrieval, auth, export, and portfolio linkage
instruments.operation: How this briefing says the vision should be accomplished, translated into a public project route. 1. Read README for project card + boundary rules. 2. Open seed_matrix.csv to see the starter rows (15-25 rows typical). 3. Run Pass 1 (verification) before expansion — no row proceeds without confirmed/TO-VERIFY label. 4. Follow Pass 2/3/4 sequence to reach 150-250 rows and then produce the playbook. 5. Apply QA checklist to every new row. Wire or preserve the implementation files: None — pure reference architecture.
